title: Production readiness
description: What 'ready to take traffic' means for a service running in this platform. Each rule maps to something the Helm chart already templates — so passing Bronze means the chart is wired up correctly, Gold means the service is properly observable and resilient.

rules.title: Exposes /healthz
rules.description: Liveness endpoint — kubelet uses this to decide whether the process is alive.

rules.title: Exposes /readyz
rules.description: Readiness endpoint — gates traffic. Required so that DB outages shed traffic instead of crashing pods.

rules.title: ArgoCD reports Healthy + Synced
rules.description: Current cluster state matches Git and the workload is healthy.

rules.title: ServiceMonitor present
rules.description: Prometheus scrapes /metrics. Without this, the service is invisible to observability.

rules.title: Has Grafana dashboard
rules.description: Pre-built dashboard ConfigMap auto-loaded into Grafana, so oncall has a single place to look.

rules.title: Has PrometheusRule
rules.description: Alerts fire when the service is down or error rate is elevated.

rules.title: PodDisruptionBudget present
rules.description: Node drains and voluntary disruptions can't take all replicas down at once.

rules.title: Runs with ≥2 replicas
rules.description: Single-replica services can't survive a node failure or rolling update without downtime.
